title: Riot Platforms' Bitcoin Mining Expansion and Operational Efficiency
text: Riot Platforms demonstrated significant operational improvements in 2025 as the company expanded its mining fleet and improved overall efficiency metrics. The company's Bitcoin production rose to 5,686 BTC in 2025 from 4,828 BTC in 2024. Fleet efficiency improved by 22% year-over-year to 21.1 J/TH, reflecting the company's successful deployment of newer, more efficient mining hardware. Despite rising global network difficulty, Riot maintained competitive power costs, averaging 4.2 cents per kilowatt hour by late 2025. The company benefited from its unique position in Texas, leveraging ERCOT's Four Coincident Peak Program for additional cost savings. Bitcoin mining revenue reached $565 million for the year, demonstrating the continued profitability of core mining operations. The company's strategic partnerships, including long-term fixed-price hash rate agreements with MicroBT, provided stability amid volatile market conditions. Management emphasized the company's focus on being a flexible power consumer, enabling it to optimize operations based on real-time power market dynamics.
